title: phyllorkis pavimentata
intro: Phyllorkis pavimentata, also known as the "Indian Tinospora", is a woody climber plant native to the tropical forests of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Phyllorkis pavimentata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Phyllorkis pavimentata include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other medicinal products.
